etymology.description: semantic 糸(糸) (mì, "silk; thread") + phonetic 臤(𰀡) (kān) -> tightness
etymology.interpretation: The character 緊(緊) (jǐn, "tight; strict; close; urgent") combines the semantic 糸(糸) (mì, "silk; thread") with the phonetic 臤(𰀡) (kān), conveying the concept of tightness as threads pulled taut are tight. This imagery naturally extends to strictness, closeness, and urgent situations, which all similarly require tension or pressure.
etymology.simplification: Simplified from 緊 (緊) by replacing 臤 with 𰀡, maintaining the original sound while simplifying the component.

examples.chinese: 他的鞋带太紧(他的鞋帶太緊)。
老板对项目的期限要求很紧(老闆對項目的期限要求很緊)。
examples.pinyin: Lǎobǎn duì xiàngmù de qīxiàn yāoqiú hěn jǐn.
examples.english: The boss is very strict with the project deadlines.

examples.chinese: 我们之间的关系很紧(我們之間的關係很緊)。
examples.pinyin: Wǒmen zhījiān de guānxì hěn jǐn.
